i. draw cash advances only for disbursements that are highly impractical for direct payment;

iii. ensure liquidation of the cash advances within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
1.32 In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and the Local
Chief Executive agreed through the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and Municipal
Accountant to implement effective monitoring of the grant and liquidation of
cash advances by: (a) ceasing the granting of cash advances to Non-Special
Disbursing Officers, instead, designate additional Special Disbursing Officers,
if necessary; (b) establishing and enforcing stricter monitoring mechanisms to
ensure all cash advances are liquidated within the prescribed time frame.
Moreover, discontinue the granting of cash advances that exceed the
maximum accountability limits. If deemed necessary, increase the fidelity
bond premiums of the accountable officers. Furthermore, ensure that the
appropriate documentary requirements are attached to the liquidation reports
before submitting to the audit team.

2.20 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed through the:
(a) Accounting and Treasury Offices to reconcile their records on RPT/SET
receivables to ensure accurate and reliable reporting; (b) OIC-Municipal
Treasurer, employ effective collection strategies by updating and posting the
notices of delinquency for property taxes at designated areas and conducting
massive campaigns and public information dissemination on the value of
paying taxes; and if warranted, enforce the provision of Section 254 (b) of the
RA No. 7160 in the collection of delinquent taxes; and (c) Municipal Assessor
to coordinate with the Provincial Government to facilitate the required
general revision of Real Property Assessments and the Schedules of Fair
Market Values, in compliance with Section 219 of the aforementioned
regulation.

3.41 In view of the aforesaid observations, we recommended and the Local
Chief Executive agreed through the (a) Municipal Accountant to recognize the
donated PTV and impairment loss for unserviceable assets in the books of
accounts; (b) property officer to perform its mandated function to (i) ensure
that all operational and roadworthy vehicles are properly registered with the
LTO and duly insured with the GSIS; (ii) facilitate the preparation and/or
reconstruction of property records and registries; (iii) establish accountability
on properties through issuance, monitoring and updating of AREs for each
recorded PPEs; (iv) expedite the assignment of property tags and property
numbers to each PPE item; and (c) Disposal Committee cause the immediate
disposal of the unserviceable properties to avert unfavorable effect thereof
caused by prolonged non-disposal following the guidelines for the disposal of
government properties provided in the recently issued Commission on Audit
(COA) and the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Joint Circular
(JC) No. 2024-1 dated January 30, 2024 re: Revised Manual on the Disposal
of Government Properties.

4.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive through the
Municipal Accounting Office (a) properly classify progress billings under the
CIP account until project completion is certified by the Engineering Office;
and (b) implement a CIP monitoring system to track ongoing infrastructure
projects and prevent premature recognition as completed assets.

5.21 In light of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief
Executive, through the Municipal Accountant: (a) ensure that source agencies
are identified, the status of PPAs assessed, and any unutilized portion of the
funds returned to the source agency, as necessary; and (b) regularly verify,
analyze, and validate the existence of payables, with proper disposition carried
out in accordance with prescribed procedures. We also recommend that
Management take advantage of the one-time cleansing of dormant accounts
provided under COA Circular No. 2023-008 to clear and properly account for
long-outstanding balances.

6.15 In view of the aforesaid observations, we recommended that the Local
Chief Executive through the: (a) Municipal Human Resource Management
Officer continue updating and reconciling leave credits of employees of the
Municipality; and (b) Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and
MHRMO develop a structured plan to integrate the recognition of Leave
Benefits Payable within the three-year transition period as prescribed under
COA Circular No. 2024-009.

7.20 During the exit conference, Management agreed with the audit
recommendations.

8.24 In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief
Executive, through the OIC-Human Resource Management Officer and
Municipal Accountant, implement strong internal controls and monitoring
mechanisms to ensure the accurate computation and payment of salaries and
wages of JOs and COS by: (a) requiring the use of DTRs as the primary basis
for payroll preparation, rather than relying on the employee logbook; (b)
ensuring proper deduction of tardiness, undertime, and absences in the
computation of salaries; (c) requiring the timely submission of complete
documentary attachments for payroll processing and liquidation of cash
advances; and (d) immediately submit the lacking documents, as discussed
above in proper form to the Office of the Auditor in order to render the
appropriate audit action thereon.

9.18 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: (a) adhere
with the guidelines set by Section 287 of R.A. No. 7160 or the Local
Government Code of 1991 and DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November
4, 2020 on the appropriation and utilization of its Development Fund; (b)
strengthen its planning and monitoring mechanisms to ensure timely project
execution; and (c) ensure that the Comprehensive Development Plan will be
updated consistent with item 3.2.1 of the aforesaid Circular.

10.26 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed through the
MPDO, MBO, Municipal Accountant and LDRRMO to: (a) strengthen
planning and monitoring to optimize the use of the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) in line with the LDRRM Plans;
(b) judiciously review and authorize transactions based only on the budgeted
amounts; and (c) ensure that the PPAs in the AIP are harmonized with the 3-
Year DRRMP consistent with Section 12 paragraph 6 of the RA No. 10121 or
the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act of 2010.

11.21 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed through the
GFPS (a) continue to adhere to the guidelines set forth under the R.A. No.
9710 PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular Nos. 2013-01 and
2016-01; (b) adopt the parameters of appropriate cost distribution and gender
analysis for its PPAs using the HGDG tools to ensure effective resource
allocation and program implementation; (c) ensure that any changes in the
GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) are properly reviewed and endorsed by the
DILG, in compliance with Section 6 of the JMC; and (d) establish stricter
monitoring and documentation processes to ensure adherence to regulatory
requirements and improve the effectiveness of gender-responsive initiatives.

12.3 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed through the
Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to continue the practice
of complying with the withholding and remittance of taxes due the BIR in
accordance with Section 114 (c) of the National Revenue Code as amended by
RA No. 9337 and Revenue Regulation No. 2-98.

13.3 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed through the
Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to continue the practice
of the strict observance of GSIS regulation on withholding of employee’s
contributions and timely remittances of employees’ and employers’
contributions in accordance with Section 6 (b) of Republic Act No. 8291 dated
May 30, 1997.

14.2 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed through the
Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to continuously observe
the practice of remitting Pag-IBIG premium contributions on a timely basis so
that the employees can enjoy all the privileges of a Pag-IBIG member.

15.5 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed through the
Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to strictly observe the practice of intact
and timely remittances of employees’ and employers’ contributions due to
PhilHealth in accordance with Section 18 of the IRR of the National Health
Insurance Act of 2013 and PhilHealth Circular No. 1, s. 2014.

16.2 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to continually
ensure observance of applicable rules and regulations in the receipt and use of
government funds and property.

We recommend that the LCE instruct (a) the HRMO to (i) formulate policies to ensure that the personnel to be hired has the proper skills and competence to deliver services required based on specific duties; (ii) execute individual contracts to which the services of the JOs and COS shall be based upon; (b) the Municipal Accountant to ensure the validity of claims for payroll by requiring complete supporting documentation prior to the payment; and (c) the Municipal Treasurer to only release wages to the personnel in payroll, otherwise, require authorization and validate the same with the proper identification/IDs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council to:
continue to appropriate, account, and report the LDRRMF properly;
conduct adequate research and planning before the preparation of LDRRMFIP; and
set a realistic and specific schedule of implementation for each program, projects and activities in the LDRRMFIP to maximize the utilization of LDRRMF.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) instruct the Municipal Development Council, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, to include in the Annual Budget for CY 2023 the funding for the second phase of MRF with requisite consideration of availability of fund; (b) require the Municipal Engineer to prepare the revised plan for the MRF and Women's Recovery Center based on the adjusted Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC); (c) direct the Municipal Budget Officer to observe the reporting and monitoring required for fund transfers from National Government; and (d) continue to comply with the guidelines on the receipt, utilization and accounting thereof.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the inventory committee to carefully plan and conduct the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances to improve the reliability of its PPE accounts, and eventually, its financial statements.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Engineer to ensure the effective and efficient implementation of development projects; (b) MPDO to (i) provide specific implementation schedule for each PPA in the AIP; (ii) in close coordination with the Local Development Council, undertake a thorough evaluation of the programs, projects and activities to be funded under the 20% Development Fund and stop from charging expenditures which do not partake the nature of investment and capital expenditures; and (iii) continue identifying high-impact capital expenditures projects consistent with the Medium-Term Development Plan of the Municipality.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the GFP to (a) carefully plan the identification of GAD PPAs and the corresponding budget allocations; (b) ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the GAD PPAs and the utilization of the GAD budget; and (c) use the HGDG tool to properly identify the gender issues and LGU PPAs or its portion that can be attributed to GAD Activities.

This notwithstanding, we recommended and the LCE agreed to continually ensure the withholding and timely remittance of taxes in conformity with Revenue Memorandum Circular Nos. 23-007 and 23-2012, and BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98.

We reiterated our previous recommendation and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to (a) coordinate with the GSIS for the reconciliation and disposition of the unidentified year-end balance; and (b) continue the monthly deduction and remittance of GSIS contributions in accordance with R.A. No. 8291.

We commended Management for the faithful compliance with the withholding and prompt remittance of premiums to the PhilHealth and recommended the continual observance thereof for the benefits of its employees.

We commended Management in complying with the provisions of R.A. No. 9679 on the withholding and prompt remittance of contributions and loan amortizations, and advised the continued observance of the requirements of the Act.
We recommended and the LCE agreed to continually ensure observance of applicable rules and regulations in the receipt and use of government funds and property.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
require the Municipal Treasurer (MT) to furnish the MA a duly signed certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible every beginning of the year; and
instruct the MA to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to recognize the CY 2020 RPT/SET receivables based on the certified list from the MTO as follows:

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to undertake the following courses of actions:
For Donations In-kind (for Calamity Purposes)
direct the officials concerned to secure a copy of the document supporting the receipt of goods or, in the absence thereof, issue acknowledgement receipt therefor before turning these over to the MDRRMO;
instruct the MDRM Officer maintain a record of donations received and issued and render a report therefor to the Accounting Office;
advise the MSWDO to submit to the Office of the COA Auditor the documents evidencing receipt and distribution of In-kind Donations for further evaluation; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant to render report on the receipt and issuance of donations-in-kind received pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2014-002 and 2020-009.
For Donations In-kind (for RHU Use)
a. 	instruct the RHU to furnish the Accounting Office a copy of RIS supporting the receipt of goods from other government agencies and issuance thereof as basis of the former in the recognition of said inventories in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
advise the Municipal Accountant to (i) draw a Journal Entry Voucher to recognize the unutilized portion of the BGCM as subsidy in the books of the municipality; (ii) coordinate with the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) in the submission of the required documents to the Office of the Auditor for further evaluation; and (iii) ensure that all the supporting documents are properly signed and provided with complete information;
instruct the BAC to strictly comply with the posting and reporting requirements for any grant received from the National Government for information purposes and transparency; and
continue the effective management of limited government funds in battling the COVID-19 pandemic.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) thru the Bids and Awards Committee, regularly update the procurement schedule of PPAs to ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund; and (b) in close coordination with the Local Development Council, undertake a thorough evaluation of the programs, projects and activities to be funded under the 20% Development Fund and stop from charging expenditures which do not partake the nature of investment and capital expenditures.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MDDRM Officer being the member of the MDRRMC to (a) incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be funded from the unexpended balance of previous years; and (b) submit to the Office of the Auditor the report on the utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the GFPS to continually apply GAD mechanisms and processes to sustain the LGU’s commitment towards promoting gender equality and women empowerment in government operations, in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC Nos. 2013-01 and 2016-01; (b) identify and implement gender-responsive PPAs taking into consideration the available avenues vis-à-vis the limitations of community quarantines; and (c) submit the documentary requirements supporting the GAD Accomplishment Report.

We commended Management for the prompt remittance of the taxes withheld during the current year to the BIR. This notwithstanding, we recommended and the LCE agreed to continually ensure the withholding and timely remittance of taxes in conformity with Revenue Memorandum Circular Nos. 23-007 and 23-2012, and BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98.

We commended Management for the faithful compliance with the withholding and prompt remittance of premiums to the PhilHealth and recommended the continual observance thereof for the benefits of its employees.

We commended Management in complying with the provisions of R.A. No. 9679 on the withholding and prompt remittance of contributions and loan amortizations, and advised the continued observance of the requirements of the Act.

We commended Management in its consistent effort to comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 8291 on the withholding and prompt remittance of contributions and loan amortizations and recommended the continuous observance of its duties and obligations under the Act.
We recommended and the LCE agreed to continually ensure observance of applicable rules and regulations in the receipt and use of government funds.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:

Municipal Accountant to adjust the misstatements in the following accounts through the following entries:
 
 


Municipal Treasurer to (i) submit to the Municipal Accountant the Report of Unreleased Checks as at end of every month; and (ii) update the RPTAR to include the accrued interests and surcharges and submit together with the Report of Delinquencies on market rentals to the Municipal Accountant every year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has submitted a Report of Unreleased Checks as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) see to it that no additional cash advance will be granted unless the previous cash advance is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made; (ii) coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the amount of cash advance granted does not exceed the maximum cash accountability covered by the fidelity bond; and (iii) require the concerned personnel to liquidate or render an accounting of their cash advances within the reglementary period; and (b) Municipal Treasurer to (i) monitor the CAs of his accountable officers with the end view of ensuring the timely liquidation of cash advances; and (ii) require the AOs to maintain cashbook for Advances to Special Disbursing Officer (1-03-05-030) account to properly identify the custody and administration of resources entrusted to them.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant verbally demanded from the AO to liquidate their cash advances. Meanwhile, he allowed grants of Cash Advance of Municipal Treasurer for Salaries and Wages and Cash Advances for Events where time is of the essence.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) cause the proper planning of PPAs to ensure the optimal use of the 20% DF; and 




(b) continue investing in high-impact capital expenditures projects and provide more development opportunities to the community.
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to continue their satisfactory compliance of the existing regulations on the use of the LDRRM Fund.

We reiterated and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) designate a person who can fully perform the functions of Municipal Environmental and Natural Resources Officer; and (b) consider extending assistance to barangays affected by the road clearing in establishing a functional Materials Recovery Facility to strengthen the implementation of solid waste management plan throughout the Municipality.


We further recommended the enforcement of the ordinance imposing garbage fees, fines and penalties relative to the implementation of R.A. No. 9003 to augment the funds dedicated for the implementation of an effective Solid Waste Management Plan.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer to (i) facilitate the execution of contracts with the 35 tenants identified; (ii) ensure that the contract duration conforms with the provision of the Market Code; and 

(iii) pursue effective strategies to collect from delinquent stallholders/space occupants; and 


(b) Municipal Accountant to (i) maintain Special Accounts for market operation in the General Fund; (ii) prepare separate financial statements therefor for ease of determining its viability and profitability; (iii) recognize in the books of accounts all receivables from uncollected market rentals and surcharges which are covered by valid contracts; and 

(iv) regularly send demand letters to delinquent stallholders/occupants to enforce collection of current and prior years’ receivables.

observations.recommendation:
1.12     We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to  instruct
        the Municipal Treasurer to:


        a)  liquidate  his  cash  advances  within  the  prescribed  period
           provided in COA Circular 97-002. Additional cash advances should
           be granted to him only after the  liquidation  of  his  previous
           cash advances;


        b) maintain the cashbook following  the  guidelines  set  forth  in
           Section 28, Volume II of Manual on the New Government Accounting
           System; and


        c)  regularly  reconcile  his  cash  records  with  the   Municipal
           Accountant.

2.7      We recommended and the Management agreed to  close  the  savings
        account and open a checking account for Nampicuan Water System  and
        ensure payment on goods and services be made through check and paid
        directly to the suppliers/contractors.

3.11     We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:


        a. require Municipal Treasurer to furnish  the  City  Accountant  a
           duly signed certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the
           amount due and collectible  every  beginning  of  the  year  for
           recording purposes;


        b. instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV  to  record  the
           RPT/SET receivables based on the certified list from the  Office
           of the City Treasurer and ensure that the  recorded  receivables
           are based on actual reference documents;


        c. encourage the involvement  of  barangay  officials  in  the  tax
           campaign to reach out to a larger area; and


        d. intensify tax collections thru formulating strategies to improve
           collection of real property taxes.

4.6      We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:


        a)  create  an  Inventory  Committee  to  undertake  the   physical
           inventory of all the PPE of the Municipality;


        b) instruct the  designated  Property  Officer  and  the  Municipal
           Accountant to exert effort to update and reconcile their records
           regularly and investigate the causes of discrepancy and make the
           necessary adjustments to come up with a  complete  and  accurate
           balance of PPE accounts in the financial statements; and


        c) instruct the designated Property Officer to prepare  and  submit
           the revised Report on Physical  Count  of  Property,  Plant  and
           Equipment as required in Section 124 of the Manual on  the  NGAS
           for LGUs, Volume I.

5.6      We  recommended  that  the  Local  Chief   Executive   initiate
        appropriate action for the titling of all the  land  owned  by  the
        Municipal Government of Nampicuan and register them in its name  to
        establish full ownership and legal rights over the property.

6.8      We recommended and the Municipal Mayor  agreed  to  observe
        strictly the general limitations on personal services  cited  under
        Section 325(a) of RA 7160 and Item 4.2 of LBC No. 98 dated  October
        14, 2011 to ensure that the  Municipality  shall  have  a  balanced
        budget and that enough funds are  allocated  for  the  delivery  of
        basic and development projects.


   6.9      We also recommended that the Municipal  Mayor  being  the  Local
        Chief  Executive  ensure  that  the  budgeting  requirements   were
        completely complied with and  payments  of  personal  services  are
        within the budget limitations to avoid expenditures  wanting  legal
        basis.

7.8      We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:


        a. properly establish the GAD Focal  Point  System  with  Technical
           Working Group and Executive Committee;


        b. send all members of the GFPS including elected  local  officials
           and department heads  to  capacity  building  programs  and  GAD
           seminars;


        c. encourage all members of the  GFPS  to  function  and  discharge
           their duties accordingly; and


        d. instruct the members of the GFPS to  (i)  prepare  proposal  for
           PPAs earlier to give proper time for their review and  approval;
           and (ii) analyze each activities for appropriate  allocation  of
           fund.

8.10     We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  (LCE)  and  other
        concerned  officials  exert  more  effort   to   obtain   ownership
        records/documents  on  the  all   insurable   properties   of   the
        municipality; complete database  of  information  thereon;  and  to
        secure adequate insurance  for  all  insurable  properties  of  the
        Municipality.

9.8      We recommended that the Management continue to adhere the  basic
        provisions of law on waste management and to give assistance to the
        barangay on the basic training to effectively use their MRFs.

10.11    We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to  require:
        (a) the Municipality’s Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)  to  prepare
        the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) to be approved by the  head
        of the procuring entity and  submit  the  same  to  the  Government
        Procurement and Policy  Board  (GPPB)  in  printed  and  electronic
        format within fourteen (14) calendar days after each semester;  and
        (b) the BAC Secretariat to post all Notices of Awards  and  Notices
        to  Proceed,  together  with  the  approved   contracts   for   the
        procurement of infrastructure projects, goods, and services in  the
        PhilGEPS, the agency website and in any conspicuous  place  in  the
        premises of the Municipality within the prescribed period  pursuant
        to the above cited provisions in the RIRR of RA No. 9184.

11.4     We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor,  thru  the  Municipal
        Planning Dev’t. Officer, continue to (a)  develop/invest  in  high-
        impact capital expenditures projects in the utilization of its  20%
        Development Fund;  and  (b)  ensure  the  effective  and  efficient
        implementation  of  the  projects  to  maximize  the  developmental
        benefits of the intended beneficiaries.
